info.title: Study App API · etr_study_app canon
info.description: Auto-generated combined view of every fragment under `openapi/test/fragments/`. Each fragment's path-item and components are inlined (component names carry an operationId prefix to avoid cross-fragment collisions; relative file refs are rebased to this document's directory). Regenerated by `tools/governance/validate_openapi.py` on every run.

post.summary: Create a new Conspectus and its initial schedule (slot A).
post.description:
Creates a new `Conspectus` row for the calling tenant and atomically
provisions its initial schedule (slot A, 1-hour cadence, revision 1).
The composite natural key is `(system_uuid, system_user_id)` — the
server rejects the call with 404 when no such user exists.

Requires `X-API-Key` (tenant auth) and `Idempotency-Key`. A repeated
request with the same key within the 24 h window replays the cached
201 response; a repeated request with the same key but a different
payload returns 409.

default.summary: Standard creation with cue_sheet + dense paragraph + bullets

value.title: Cue-based retrieval — first pass
value.dense_paragraph: Extract-Transform-Retrieve is a spaced-retrieval discipline where the learner distils a source into a cue sheet, a dense paragraph, and a bullet list, then retrieves them at policy-driven intervals.

201.description: Created — the persisted conspectus with its initial schedule.

Location.description: Canonical URL of the newly created conspectus.

get.description:
Returns the learner's conspectuses, paginated with an opaque cursor
for stable forward-only traversal across large catalogues. Distinct
from `listDueConspectuses`, which is a scan capped at 100 items for
the study-load widget — this operation is the full library view.

Sort is fixed at `created_at DESC, conspectus_uuid DESC` in v1.
Filters (all optional, combinable):

* `slot` — restrict to a single ETR slot.
* `created_after` / `created_before` — half-open range on creation
  timestamp; both server-parsed as RFC 3339, UTC recommended.
* `include_invalid` — when `false` (default), soft-deleted rows
  (`is_row_invalid=1`) are hidden. Set `true` to include them for
  audit / restore workflows.

Read-only; no `Idempotency-Key`. Requires `X-API-Key`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque forward cursor from the `next_cursor` field of the previous response. Absent on the first page. The server rejects malformed / expired cursors with 422.

parameters.name: slot
parameters.description: Filter to a single ETR slot.

parameters.name: include_invalid
parameters.description: When `true`, soft-deleted rows are included in the results.

first_page.summary: First page with two items and a cursor for more

422.description: Unprocessable Content — query parameters failed validation.

errors.message: Query parameter `cursor` is malformed or has expired. Restart pagination without it.
Query parameter `limit` must be between 1 and 100.
Query parameter `created_after` must be earlier than `created_before`.

get.summary: Return conspectuses whose next review is due.
get.description:
Returns conspectuses owned by `(system_uuid, system_user_id)` whose
`next_review_at <= due_before` (default: server `now()`), ordered
by `next_review_at ASC, created_at ASC, conspectus_uuid ASC`.
Read-only; no `Idempotency-Key`.

Hard-capped at 100 items in v1 — no cursor / pagination. A learner
expected to overflow 100 pending reviews in a single call is a red
flag for policy configuration, not a paging problem.

Requires `X-API-Key` (tenant auth).

parameters.name: due_before
parameters.description: Upper bound for `next_review_at`; RFC 3339, must be timezone-aware (UTC recommended). Server default: `now()` in UTC.

parameters.name: slot
parameters.description: Filter results to a single ETR slot.

200.description: OK — the due conspectuses (may be empty).

one_due.summary: One conspectus in slot A is due

422.description: Unprocessable Content — query params failed validation.

errors.message: Query parameter `slot` must be one of A, B, C, D.

malformed_due_before.summary: `due_before` is not a valid RFC 3339 timestamp

errors.message: Query parameter `due_before` must be an RFC 3339 timezone-aware timestamp.

delete.description:
Marks the conspectus as invalidated by setting `is_row_invalid=1`,
`invalidation_reason_uuid`, and `invalidated_at`. The row is
retained in storage — subsequent `listDueConspectuses` calls hide
it, but `getConspectusById` still returns it (and `listConspectuses`
can opt-in via `include_invalid=true`).

**Cascade rule.** Linked error-log entries have their
`conspectus_uuid` cleared to `null` (ON DELETE SET NULL, mirrored in
`createErrorLog`) — the errors themselves survive because they
capture learner-authored context that is valuable independent of
the linked note.

**Rationale for soft (not hard).** Learner-authored content has a
retention obligation the API respects. Hard-delete is a separate
administrative operation (out of scope in v1). Soft-delete is
idempotent by nature — repeated calls with the same body return
the already-invalidated row.

Requires `X-API-Key` (tenant auth) and `Idempotency-Key`.

parameters.name: conspectus_uuid
parameters.description: Server-generated stable identifier of the conspectus to soft-delete.

200.description: OK — the soft-deleted conspectus. Idempotent on repeat.

value.message: `Idempotency-Key` header is required on this operation.

get.summary: Look up a single Conspectus by uuid.
get.description:
Returns the full `ConspectusResponse` for the given uuid, provided
the row is owned by the calling tenant. Read-only; no
`Idempotency-Key`. Requires `X-API-Key`.

Cross-tenant misses collapse into 404 (never 403) so the API
doesn't leak existence of rows owned by other tenants.

Rows soft-invalidated by `deleteConspectus` are still returned by
this operation — the client can inspect `is_row_invalid` and
`invalidation_reason_uuid` to decide what to render.

patch.summary: Partial content update on an existing Conspectus.
patch.description:
Updates ETR content fields (`title`, `cue_sheet`, `dense_paragraph`,
`bullets`) on an existing Conspectus. At least one of the four must
be present in the body; otherwise the server returns 400
`CONSPECTUS_PATCH_BODY_EMPTY`. `cue_sheet` is whole-replaced when
present, not field-level-merged.

Schedule fields (`slot`, `next_review_at`, `schedule_revision`) are
never touched by this operation — use `reviewConspectus` for that.
`content_version` is incremented by exactly 1 on success.

Requires `X-API-Key` (tenant auth) and `Idempotency-Key`. Last-writer
wins on concurrent patches (no optimistic-concurrency guard on
content — only on schedule).

parameters.name: conspectus_uuid
parameters.description: Server-generated stable identifier of the conspectus to patch.

prompts: Why is the cue sheet retrieved before the paragraph?

200.description: OK — the updated conspectus. `content_version` incremented; schedule unchanged.

post.summary: Record a review outcome and advance the schedule.
post.description:
Applies a retrieve outcome (`easy` | `hard` | `forgot`) to the
conspectus's schedule, advancing the ETR slot ladder per the
`SchedulePolicy` version pinned on the schedule row. Atomically
updates the schedule row (new `slot`, `next_review_at`,
`schedule_revision + 1`) and appends an immutable review log entry.

The reference policy (see ADR 0039):

| From \ Outcome | easy   | hard | forgot |
|----------------|--------|------|--------|
| A              | B (1D) | A (1H) | A (1H) |
| B              | C (3D) | A (1H) | A (1H) |
| C              | D[0] (7D) | B (1D) | A (1H) |
| D[n]           | D[n+1] (…60D at [3]) | C (3D) | A (1H) |

Requires `X-API-Key` (tenant auth) and `Idempotency-Key`.
Optionally supply `expected_schedule_revision` for optimistic
concurrency — mismatch → 409 `CONSPECTUS_REVIEW_REVISION_CONFLICT`.

parameters.name: conspectus_uuid
parameters.description: Server-generated stable identifier of the conspectus to review.

hard.summary: Retrieve was hard — retreat by one step

forgot.summary: Retrieve failed — reset to slot A

200.description: OK — the conspectus with its advanced schedule.

get.summary: Return the immutable audit log of a Conspectus (reviews + content patches).
get.description:
Returns two interleaved streams for the given conspectus:

* `reviews` — every `reviewConspectus` call, with slot transition
  (`slot_from` → `slot_to`), outcome tag, resulting
  `schedule_revision`, and server timestamp.
* `content_events` — every `patchConspectus` call, with the fields
  that changed (`changed_fields`) and the resulting `content_version`.

Ordered `created_at ASC, sequence ASC` (chronological). Cursor-
paginated with the same `limit` semantics as `listConspectuses`.
Filters:

* `event_type` — `review` | `content_patch` | `all` (default).
* `since` — inclusive lower bound on `created_at` (RFC 3339); most
  integrators paginate by anchoring at the last-seen event's
  `created_at` after a reconnect.

Read-only; no `Idempotency-Key`. Requires `X-API-Key`.

Both streams are append-only: no update, no delete. The endpoint
is the canonical audit trail for the conspectus.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque forward cursor from the `next_cursor` field of the previous response.

200.description: OK — a page of audit events.

404.description: Not Found — either the user or the conspectus does not exist for this tenant.

user_not_found.summary: No user matches the composite key

value.message: User with this `system_user_id` and `system_uuid` was not found.

conspectus_not_found.summary: No conspectus with the given uuid for the caller

value.message: Conspectus not found for the calling tenant.

422.description: Unprocessable Content — query parameters failed validation.

errors.message: Query parameter `event_type` must be one of review, content_patch, all.

post.summary: Append an error log entry for a learner (optionally linked to a conspectus or review).
post.description:
Records a mistake the learner made during retrieval so it can be
surfaced as remediation context on the linked conspectus. The row
is append-only — no PATCH, no DELETE in v1.

Both `conspectus_uuid` and `review_log_id` are optional. When
present, the server verifies each references a row owned by the
calling tenant; a missing or foreign reference returns 404
`ERROR_LOG_REFERENCE_NOT_FOUND`.

`message` is PII class **High** — full server-side redaction is
applied to log streams, and the operation is expected to obey the
retention window defined for user-authored content.

Requires `X-API-Key` (tenant auth) and `Idempotency-Key`.

value.message: I confused 'Retrieve' with 'Recall' — need to re-read the ETR loop.

get.summary: List a learner's error log entries (optionally filtered by conspectus).
get.description:
Returns the learner's error log entries ordered
`created_at DESC, error_uuid DESC`, capped at 100 items. When
`conspectus_uuid` is present, results are filtered to entries
linked to that conspectus — the canonical shape for the in-note
remediation sidebar.

Read-only; no `Idempotency-Key`. Requires `X-API-Key`.

`message` is PII class **High** — treat responses as sensitive on
every hop.

parameters.description: Filter results to errors linked to this conspectus.

get.description:
Returns the learner's review streak, computed from
`conspectus_review_logs` grouped by UTC calendar date. The current
streak is alive today (or yesterday — a 24-hour grace) and counts
back while every prior day carries at least one review. The longest
streak is the largest such run on record. `goal_days` is the fixed
30-day milestone the client celebrates.

Read-only; no `Idempotency-Key`. Requires `X-API-Key`. Each call
recomputes — no caching in v1.

404.description: Not Found — the user does not exist for this tenant.

errors.message: Query parameter `system_uuid` is required.

get.summary: Recap of the previous day's review activity.
get.description:
Returns yesterday's digest for the learner: how many reviews landed,
how many were scheduled, the share of `easy` outcomes as a rounded
percentage, and how many scheduled reviews were missed. The client
renders it as the one-line «how did yesterday go» strip and picks a
tone (success / warn / danger) from `accuracy_pct`.

Read-only; no `Idempotency-Key`. Requires `X-API-Key`.

200.description: OK — digest for the previous UTC calendar day.

good_day.summary: Nine of ten reviewed with high accuracy

errors.message: Query parameter `system_uuid` is required.

get.summary: Per-day review counts for the trailing N days (heat-map source).
get.description:
Returns one bucket per UTC calendar day for the trailing `days`
window, zero-filled — a day with no reviews is present with
`count: 0`, so the client renders a gap-free grid. Each bucket
also carries a pre-computed `intensity` (0–4) so heat-map cell
shading needs no client-side maths.

Read-only; no `Idempotency-Key`. Requires `X-API-Key`.

parameters.name: days
parameters.description: Size of the trailing window in days.

200.description: OK — zero-filled per-day buckets, oldest first.

three_days.summary: Three-day window with one quiet day

errors.message: Query parameter `days` must be between 1 and 365.

get.summary: Return a server-shuffled lookahead of due conspectuses (evening-review shape).
get.description:
Returns the conspectuses due within a configurable time window,
pre-shuffled by the server. The intended consumer is the
«evening review» client flow, which wants presentation-ready
randomization without the client seeing the full due-list.

**Server-side randomization** — a decision, not an accident. The
client never receives items that fall outside the window, and
never has to implement its own shuffle (which is a common source
of subtle bugs — biased Fisher-Yates, insecure PRNG, inconsistent
cross-device order). The server owns the shuffle and stamps each
item with a stable `preview_order_index` so the client can render
deterministically even after a mid-session refresh.

**Determinism.** The response is deterministic for a given
`(system_user_id, system_uuid, window, random_seed)` tuple within
the current `computed_at` minute — subsequent calls with the same
seed replay the same order. When `random_seed` is omitted, the
server generates one and echoes it in the response so the client
can pin its session against it.

Read-only; no `Idempotency-Key`. Requires `X-API-Key`.

parameters.name: window
parameters.description: ISO 8601 duration bound on `next_review_at <= computed_at + window`. Server accepts `PT1H`, `PT4H`, `PT24H`, `P1D`; other durations are rejected with 422.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum items in the preview. Default `20`.

parameters.name: random_seed
parameters.description: Optional deterministic-shuffle seed. Alphanumeric string, 1–64 chars. When omitted, the server generates one and echoes it in the response.

200.description: OK — server-shuffled preview of due items in the window.

three_items.summary: Three items due in the next 4 hours, server-shuffled

404.description: Not Found — the user does not exist for this tenant.

user_not_found.summary: No user matches the composite key

value.message: User with this `system_user_id` and `system_uuid` was not found.

422.description: Unprocessable Content — query parameters failed validation.

errors.message: Query parameter `window` must be one of PT1H, PT4H, PT24H, P1D.
Query parameter `random_seed` must match `^[A-Za-z0-9_-]+$`.

get.summary: Aggregated study-load counts for a learner (by slot + due-now + due next 24h).
get.description:
Returns server-computed counts of the learner's active conspectuses
grouped by ETR slot (A/B/C/D), plus how many are due at snapshot
time and how many become due within the next 24 hours. The intended
consumer is the study-load badge on the client — a fast, small
payload that avoids a full `listDueConspectuses` scan.

Read-only; no `Idempotency-Key`. Requires `X-API-Key`. Snapshot-
isolated reads; no caching in v1 — each call recomputes.

mid_session.summary: Mid-session snapshot with items in three slots

post.description:
Creates a new `User` row for the calling tenant. Uniqueness is
enforced on the composite natural key `(system_user_id, system_uuid)`.
The server generates a stable `client_uuid` used as the owner key on
every downstream resource.

Requires the `X-API-Key` header (tenant auth) and the
`Idempotency-Key` header. A repeated request with the same key
within the 24 h idempotency window replays the cached response.
Reuse with a different payload returns 409.

422.description: Unprocessable Content — one or more request fields failed validation.

errors.message: system_user_id must be between 1 and 36 characters.
full_name is required.

invalid_system_uuid.summary: system_uuid is not a valid UUID

errors.message: system_uuid must be a valid UUID.

invalid_timezone.summary: timezone is not a valid IANA zone

errors.message: timezone must be a valid IANA name (e.g. 'UTC', 'Europe/Moscow').

get.summary: Get user by system_uuid and system_user_id.
get.description:
Returns the user profile identified by the composite natural key
`(system_uuid, system_user_id)`. This is a pure read — no
`Idempotency-Key` is required.

Requires the `X-API-Key` header (tenant auth). Returns 404 when no
user matches the composite key for the calling tenant.

parameters.name: system_user_id
parameters.description: External user id in the source system.

patch.summary: Partial update of user profile fields.
patch.description:
Updates only the fields present in the JSON body; omitted fields
stay unchanged. The body must include at least one property —
an empty object returns 400 with `USER_102 · USER_PATCH_BODY_EMPTY`.
Extra properties are rejected (schema `additionalProperties: false`,
mirrors `model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")`).

Requires `X-API-Key` (tenant auth) and `Idempotency-Key` (write
deduplication). A repeated request with the same key within the
24 h window replays the cached response; a repeated request with
the same key but a different payload returns 409 Conflict.

Use `PUT` for a full replacement of mutable fields — see
[`updateUserBySystemUserId`](./updateUserBySystemUserId.yaml).

parameters.name: system_user_id
parameters.description: External user id in the source system.

soft_invalidate.summary: Mark row invalid with a reason

200.description: OK — the persisted user after patch.

400.description: Bad Request — business-rule failure on User patch.

value.message: PATCH body must include at least one field to update.

422.description: Unprocessable Content — one or more request fields failed validation.

errors.message: Field `timezone` must be a valid IANA timezone.

put.summary: Full replacement of mutable user profile fields.
put.description:
Replaces every mutable profile field on the user identified by the
composite natural key `(system_uuid, system_user_id)`. `full_name`
and `timezone` are required; other fields default or clear.

Requires `X-API-Key` (tenant auth) and `Idempotency-Key` (write
deduplication). A repeated request with the same key within the
24 h window replays the cached response; a repeated request with
the same key but a different payload returns 409 Conflict.

Use `PATCH` for a partial update — see
[`patchUserBySystemUserId`](./patchUserBySystemUserId.yaml).

parameters.name: system_user_id
parameters.description: External user id in the source system.

200.description: OK — the persisted user after update.

422.description: Unprocessable Content — one or more request fields failed validation.

errors.message: Field `full_name` is required.
Field `timezone` must be a valid IANA timezone.

cue_sheet.description: Cue prompts the learner will retrieve against. Must be a JSON object (not an array). Concrete shape is intentionally open in v1 — see ADR 0037 for the cue_sheet enforcement policy.

dense_paragraph.description: Dense synthesis of the source material.

bullets.description: Ordered list of atomic bullets distilling the source.

cue_sheet_schema_version.description: Version of the cue_sheet schema this row is stored under.

slot.description: Current retrieval slot in the ETR ladder.

slot_d_ladder_index.description: Position within slot D's ladder (0 immediately after entering D).

next_review_at.description: Timestamp when the next review is due (RFC 3339, UTC).

schedule_policy_id.description: Identifier of the SchedulePolicy version pinned at creation time (see ADR 0039).

algorithm_version.description: Semver of the scheduling algorithm implementation.

schedule_revision.description: Optimistic-concurrency token for the schedule row; incremented on every review.

content_version.description: Optimistic-concurrency token for the content row; incremented on every patch.

updated_at.description: Last content update timestamp; equals `created_at` on a fresh row.

schedule_updated_at.description: Last schedule update timestamp; equals `created_at` on a fresh row.

deleteConspectus_ConspectusDeleteRequest.description: Soft-delete payload. Reason is required — a delete without a traceable reason blocks audit workflows and is refused.

invalidation_reason_uuid.description: FK to a stable invalidation-reason row (e.g. «user request», «stale content», «duplicate»). Required so soft-deletes stay explainable in audit exports.

conspectus_uuid.description: Echo of the path parameter for client-side sanity checks.

items.description: Events in this page, in chronological order.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to fetch the next page. `null` when no more.

count.description: Number of events in this page.

getConspectusHistory_ConspectusHistoryEv.description: Discriminated union — `review` events carry the `review` object, `content_patch` events carry the `content_patch` object. The other payload is absent (not set to null) so clients can switch on `event_type` cleanly.

event_type.description: Discriminator; drives which payload is present.

event_id.description: Stable server-generated identifier for the event.

created_at.description: Server-side timestamp of the event (RFC 3339, UTC).

actor.description: External identity of the caller who triggered the event.

changed_fields.description: Which top-level ETR fields were replaced by the patch.

content_version_after.description: `content_version` of the row after the patch was applied.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor to fetch the next page. `null` when there are no more pages. Prefer the `next_cursor` field over inspecting `items.length` when deciding whether to keep paging — the two can disagree if the caller's `limit` matches the last page exactly.

count.description: Number of items in this page (equal to `items.length`).

patchConspectus_ConspectusPatchRequest.description: Partial content update. Server rejects the request with `CONS_102 · CONSPECTUS_PATCH_BODY_EMPTY` when none of `title`, `cue_sheet`, `dense_paragraph`, `bullets` are present.

title.description: New title; omit to leave unchanged; set null to clear.

cue_sheet.description: New cue sheet — whole-replace (not field-level merge). Omit to leave unchanged.

dense_paragraph.description: New dense paragraph; omit to leave unchanged.

bullets.description: New bullet list — whole-replace. Omit to leave unchanged.

tag.description: Retrieve outcome. `easy` advances the ladder; `hard` retreats by one step; `forgot` resets to slot A (see business rules in the operation description).

expected_schedule_revision.description: Optional optimistic-concurrency token. If present, the server compares it to the row's `schedule_revision`; mismatch → 409. Omit for last-writer-wins semantics.

reviewed_at.description: Client-side timestamp when the review happened. Currently ignored by the server (documentation only) but reserved for future offline-catchup use cases. RFC 3339, UTC.

message.description: Free-form learner-authored description of the mistake. PII class High — full server-side redaction on log streams; retention governed by the user-content policy.

conspectus_uuid.description: Optional FK to the conspectus this mistake was made against. ON DELETE SET NULL — deleting the conspectus doesn't hard-delete its errors.

review_log_id.description: Optional FK to the specific review log entry this mistake was observed on. Coexists with `conspectus_uuid` — a mistake can reference either, both, or neither.

error_uuid.description: Server-generated stable identifier for the error log entry.

message.description: Echoed learner-authored description. PII class High.

conspectus_uuid.description: Referenced conspectus, or `null` if the error is freestanding.

review_log_id.description: Referenced review log entry id, or `null`.

error_uuid.description: Server-generated stable identifier for the error log entry.

message.description: Learner-authored description of the mistake. PII class High.

review_log_id.description: Linked review log entry id, or `null`.

current_days.description: Consecutive review days ending today or yesterday (24 h grace).

longest_days.description: Longest streak on record for this learner.

computed_at.description: Server timestamp when the streak was computed (RFC 3339, UTC).

accuracy_pct.description: Share of `easy` reviews vs. total, rounded to a percent.

missed.description: Scheduled reviews that did not happen.

date.description: The UTC calendar day the digest covers.

computed_at.description: Server timestamp when the digest was computed (RFC 3339, UTC).

days.description: One bucket per UTC calendar day, oldest first, zero-filled.

date.description: UTC calendar day this bucket covers.

intensity.description: Bucketed density 0–4 for heat-map cell shading.

computed_at.description: Server timestamp when the window was computed (RFC 3339, UTC).

window.description: Echo of the effective window (default resolved server-side).

computed_at.description: Server timestamp when the preview was materialised (RFC 3339, UTC).

random_seed.description: Echo of the seed used for the shuffle. Present even when the client omitted the parameter, so the client can pin the same order for the rest of its session.

items.description: Preview items in the server-computed order.

getSchedulePreview_SchedulePreviewItem.description: Compact projection of a conspectus for the preview flow. Full content is fetched via `getConspectusById` when the client is ready to render the review card.

preview_order_index.description: Position of this item in the shuffled order (0-indexed). Stable for the given `(random_seed, computed_at)` — the client can render deterministically on session refresh.

title.description: Optional title, echoed for lightweight rendering.

next_review_at.description: When this item became due (already ≤ `computed_at + window`).

by_slot.description: Count of active conspectuses per slot. All four keys are always present; a slot with no items is `0` (not omitted). Convention simplifies client code.

total.description: Convenience total — equals the sum of `by_slot` values. Present so the client doesn't have to add four numbers just to render «14 items in your queue».

computed_at.description: Server timestamp when the aggregation was computed (RFC 3339, UTC).

system_user_id.description: User ID in the source system; unique together with `system_uuid`.

system_uuid.description: UUID of the source system (`systems.system_uuid`); composite key with `system_user_id`.

invalidation_reason_uuid.description: FK to invalidation reason, if the row is being marked invalid at creation time.

client_uuid.description: Server-generated internal identifier; stable across the user's lifetime.

invalidation_reason_uuid.description: FK to invalidation reason, if set.

system_user_id.description: External system user id, echoed from the request.

system_uuid.description: Related system UUID, echoed from the request.

username.description: Optional display username, echoed from the request.

full_name.description: Full name, echoed from the request.

timezone.description: IANA timezone string, echoed from the request.

client_uuid.description: Server-generated internal identifier; stable across the user's lifetime.

invalidation_reason_uuid.description: FK to invalidation reason, if set.

system_user_id.description: External system user id, echoed from the path.

system_uuid.description: Related system UUID, echoed from the path.

timezone.description: IANA timezone string stored for the user.

patchUserBySystemUserId_UserPatchRequest.description: Partial update payload. Every field is optional; at least one field must be present (`minProperties: 1`). Extra properties are rejected (`additionalProperties: false`, mirrors `extra="forbid"`).

full_name.description: Full name; omit to leave unchanged.

timezone.description: IANA timezone; omit to leave unchanged.

username.description: Username or login; set null to clear.

system_uuid.description: Related system UUID; set null to clear.

invalidation_reason_uuid.description: Invalidation reason UUID; set null to clear.

client_uuid.description: Server-generated internal identifier; stable across the user's lifetime.

invalidation_reason_uuid.description: FK to invalidation reason, if set.

timezone.description: IANA timezone string stored for the user.

username.description: Username or login; set null to clear.

system_uuid.description: Related system UUID; set null to clear.

invalidation_reason_uuid.description: FK to invalidation reason; set null to clear.

client_uuid.description: Server-generated internal identifier; stable across the user's lifetime.

invalidation_reason_uuid.description: FK to invalidation reason, if set.

timezone.description: IANA timezone string stored for the user.
